summary: codebuff-cli impersonates CodebuffAI and drops a mutable binary from an unrelated GitHub account
details: The package name and README impersonate the official `codebuff` tool published by CodebuffAI (README links to codebuff.com and github.com/CodebuffAI/codebuff, instructing users to `npm install -g codebuff`). However, the shipped CLI wrapper at cli/bin/codebuff.cjs hardcodes `REPO = 'Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli'` — a personal GitHub account unrelated to CodebuffAI — and on every invocation queries `/releases/latest` (a mutable tag, not pinned to the npm package version), downloads `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` to `~/.codebuff/bin/`, `chmod 0755`s it, and spawns it with the user's argv. No checksum or signature is verified, so the personal-account publisher can rotate the binary contents at any time across all installs. Additionally, the spawned binary is launched with `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` in its environment, disabling TLS certificate verification for any outbound TLS calls the binary makes — exposing credentials and API traffic to MITM. The combination of name-impersonation of a legitimate publisher, fetch of an opaque binary from a mismatched-publisher GitHub account via a mutable `latest` tag with no integrity check, and TLS-verification disablement constitutes an installer-harm dropper, not a legitimate prebuild-install pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
